zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Rebecca Notley於1594年出生在英國時，她的父親William Notley44歲，母親Ann Jane Robinson24歲。她於1611年與Thomas Maddox在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1686年在美國維吉尼亞州詹姆斯 希迪加斯托享年92歲。

nameMeanings.name: Notley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from one or more of the various places called Notley or Nutley in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Essex, Hampshire, Hertfordshire, or Sussex. The placenames mean ‘nut wood’, from Old English hnutu ‘nut’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: Rebecca
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from the Latin form of the Hebrew name Rebekah, borne by the wife of Isaac, who was the mother of Esau and Jacob (Genesis 24–27). The Hebrew root occurs in the Bible only in the vocabulary word marbek ‘cattle stall’, and its connection with the name is doubtful. In any case, Rebecca was Aramean, and the name probably has a source in Aramaic. It has always been common as a Jewish name; in England and elsewhere it began to be used also by Christians from the 14th century onwards and especially at the time of the Reformation, when Old Testament names became popular. It was very common among the Puritans in the 17th century, and has enjoyed a tremendous vogue in England since the latter part of the 20th century, among people of many different creeds. In Scotland this is found as an Anglicized form of Beathag .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: On December 6, 1606, the journey to Virginia began on three ships: the Susan Constant, the Godspeed, and the Discovery. In 1607, 104 English men and boys arrived in North America to start a settlement. On May 13 they picked Jamestown, Virginia for their settlement, which was named after their King, James I. The settlement became the first permanent English settlement in North America... In 1698, fire struck Jamestown. The fire was evidently started by a prisoner awaiting execution in the nearby prison. The fire destroyed the prison and the statehouse, though many of the public records were saved. In 1699, the government and capital were moved from Jamestown to Middle Plantation, renamed Williamsburg. People continued to live on Jamestown Island and owned farm lands, but it ceased to be a town.
historicEvents.urlTitle: National Park Service - Historic Jamestowne - A Short History of Jamestown

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Rebecca Notley was born in 1594, in England, her father, William Notley, was 44 and her mother, Ann Jane Robinson, was 24. She married Thomas Maddox in 1611, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1686, in Jamestown, James City, Virginia, United States, at the age of 92.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Rebecca Notley(이)가 1594년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Notley(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ann Jane Robinson(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1611년 영국 런던에서 Thomas Maddox(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1686년 미국 버지니아 주 자메스 시티 자메스타운에서 92세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Rebecca Notley родилась в 1594 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени William Notley было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Ann Jane Robinson, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Maddox в 1611 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1686 г. в местности джеместоун, джемесь сити, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 92 лет.
